title: 13 Best Herbal Teas For Muscle Pain
intro: Herbal teas for muscle pain are a type of medicinal tea made from herbs, plants, and flowers that have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, used to relieve muscle tension, stiffness, and soreness. These teas can treat muscle pain due to their ability to reduce inflammation, relax muscles, and improve blood circulation. Examples of herbal teas that can be used to treat muscle pain include ginger tea, which reduces inflammation and relaxes muscles, turmeric tea, which has anti-inflammatory properties, and willow bark tea, which contains salicin, a natural pain reliever similar to aspirin. Other herbal teas that can help with muscle pain include peppermint tea, which relaxes muscles and improves circulation, chamomile tea, which reduces anxiety and promotes relaxation, and rosemary tea, which improves circulation and reduces muscle spasms.

preparations.herb_desc: Zingiber officinale teas, also known as ginger tea, have been traditionally used to treat muscle pain ailments due to their anti-inflammatory, analgesic, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of ginger tea, such as gingerols and shogaols, help to reduce muscle inflammation and pain by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines. The anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties of ginger tea make it an effective natural remedy for treating muscle pain, reducing muscle spasms, and promoting relaxation. By consuming ginger tea, individuals can experience relief from muscle pain and discomfort, as well as other benefits such as improved digestion and reduced nausea.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 tablespoon of dried ginger root and 1 cup of boiling water.
Peel the ginger root and chop it into small pieces.
Steep the ginger pieces in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and add honey to taste if needed.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day as needed for muscle pain relief.
preparations.preparation_study: According to the study, Zingiber officinale teas may be effective in managing muscle pain, particularly sciatica, due to its anti-inflammatory, analgesic activity, and antinociceptive properties.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Zingiber officinale teas can be an effective herbal remedy for treating muscle pain, but possible side effects may include nausea, dizziness, and stomach upset due to its high ginger content, which can also interact with certain medications and worsen conditions such as ulcers or acid reflux. To use Zingiber officinale teas safely, it is recommended to start with a low dose and gradually increase as needed, to be consumed in moderation, especially on an empty stomach and at least 30 minutes before eating a meal.
preparation_usage: Consult doctor before using Zingiber officinale tea for muscle pain
Drink 1 cup of Zingiber officinale tea 2-3 times a day
Use 1-2 teaspoons of dried Zingiber officinale root per cup of boiling water
Steep for 5-7 minutes and strain before drinking
Continue using for 7-10 days to experience relief from muscle pain

preparations.herb_desc: Valeriana officinalis teas have been traditionally used to treat muscle pain due to their sedative, anti-inflammatory, and analgesic properties, which help to alleviate discomfort and reduce muscle spasms. The bioactive constituents of this herbal preparation, including valerenic acid, isovalerenic acid, and valepotriates, contribute to its muscle pain-relieving effects by modulating the activity of neurotransmitters and inhibiting the release of pain-causing chemicals. By promoting relaxation, reducing muscle tension, and suppressing pain signals, Valeriana officinalis teas help to treat muscle pain effectively, providing relief from conditions such as fibromyalgia and myalgia. The benefits of using Valeriana officinalis teas to treat muscle pain include reduced muscle spasms, improved sleep quality, and a decrease in the reliance on pharmaceutical pain medications.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of boiling water and 1 teaspoon of dried Valeriana officinalis root.
Measure 1 teaspoon of dried root and place it in a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup.
Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the dried root and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the root. You can add honey or lemon to taste.
Drink the tea 1-2 times a day, as needed, to help with muscle pain relief.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Valeriana officinalis teas can cause drowsiness and interact with certain medications, such as sedatives and antidepressants, which may exacerbate muscle pain or worsen underlying conditions. To use Valeriana officinalis teas safely, it is recommended to start with small doses and gradually increase as needed, and to avoid consuming them with other substances that may enhance their sedative effects.
preparation_usage: Consult a doctor before using Valeriana officinalis for muscle pain treatment.
Take 1-2 teaspoons of dried Valeriana officinalis in a cup of boiling water.
Steep for 5-10 minutes, then strain the liquid.
Drink 1-2 cups of the tea 2-3 times a day for muscle pain relief.
Use for 7-10 days, then reassess and adjust treatment plan if needed.

preparations.herb_desc: Curcuma longa teas have been traditionally used to treat muscle pain ailments due to their anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and analgesic properties. These properties help to reduce muscle inflammation and pain by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines, thereby alleviating symptoms associated with conditions such as arthritis and fibromyalgia. The bioactive constituents of Curcuma longa teas, including curcumin, demethoxycurcumin, and bisdemethoxycurcumin, are responsible for these therapeutic effects, which help to reduce muscle pain and inflammation. The benefits of using Curcuma longa teas to treat muscle pain include reduced reliance on pain medication, improved joint mobility, and enhanced overall well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of water and 1 teaspoon of dried Curcuma longa roots.
Boil the water in a pot and add the dried Curcuma longa roots.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture and discard the Curcuma longa roots.
Drink the tea warm or let it cool down before consumption.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Inflammation research : official journal of the European Histamine Research Society ... [et al.]", Curcuma longa teas for muscle pain may be effective due to the presence of curcumin and other compounds that exhibit potent anti-inflammatory activity at the hH4R receptor, with the crude extract of curcuma showing a K i value of 0.50-0.81 µg/mL.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Curcuma longa teas can be an effective treatment for muscle pain, but some users may experience side effects such as stomach upset, nausea, and diarrhea due to its active compound curcumin, which can be irritating to the digestive system. Precautions to take when using Curcuma longa teas for muscle pain include drinking plenty of water to stay hydrated, starting with small doses to assess tolerance, and avoiding consumption if you have a history of stomach ulcers or other gastrointestinal issues.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Curcuma longa tea 2 times a day.
Take 1 teaspoon of Curcuma longa powder 3 times a day.
Mix 1 teaspoon of Curcuma longa powder with 1 tablespoon of oil for massage.
Use Curcuma longa tea or oil for 5-7 days to see results.
Consult a doctor if muscle pain persists after 7 days of usage.

preparations.herb_desc: Lavandula angustifolia teas have been traditionally used to treat muscle pain due to their anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and relaxing properties, which help to soothe and calm muscle spasms. The bioactive constituents of Lavandula angustifolia teas, including linalool and linalyl acetate, have been shown to have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, making them effective in reducing muscle pain. By promoting relaxation and reducing muscle tension, Lavandula angustifolia teas help to alleviate muscle pain and improve overall well-being. The benefits of using Lavandula angustifolia teas to treat muscle pain include reduced muscle spasms, improved sleep quality, and enhanced overall relaxation and calmness.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Lavandula angustifolia flowers and 1 cup of boiling water.
Place 1 tablespoon of dried flowers in a heat-resistant cup.
Pour boiling water over the flowers and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into another cup using a piece of cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve.
Drink the tea warm or at room temperature, 2-3 times a day as needed for muscle pain relief.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Oxidative medicine and cellular longevity", Lavandula angustifolia teas may provide relief from muscle pain due to the presence of biologically active chemical constituents with significant spasmolytic effects.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Lavandula angustifolia teas can be used to alleviate muscle pain, but it may cause side effects such as dizziness, headaches, and stomach upset in some individuals, particularly when consumed in large quantities or combined with other medications. To use Lavandula angustifolia teas safely for muscle pain, it is recommended to start with small amounts, drink plenty of water, and avoid consuming it before bedtime to minimize potential interactions with other activities.
preparation_usage: Steep 1-2 teaspoons of dried Lavandula angustifolia in 1 cup of boiling water.
Drink 1-2 cups of tea 2-3 times a day as needed.
Start with low dosage and increase as needed and under doctor's guidance.
Use Lavandula angustifolia tea for 7-10 days for muscle pain relief.
Combine with stretching and massage for better muscle pain relief results.

preparations.herb_desc: Ginkgo biloba teas have been traditionally used to treat muscle pain, specifically in cases of fibromyalgia and muscle strain. The analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties of this herbal preparation help to reduce pain and inflammation, thereby alleviating muscle discomfort. The bioactive constituents, including flavonoids and terpenoids, particularly ginkgolides and bilobalide, contribute to its therapeutic effects by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory mediators and promoting blood flow to the affected areas. Regular consumption of Ginkgo biloba teas has been found to provide relief from muscle pain, improve sleep quality, and enhance overall physical well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 tablespoon of dried Ginkgo biloba leaves, a tea infuser, and a cup.
Boil 1 cup of water in a kettle or on the stove.
Place the Ginkgo biloba leaves in the tea infuser and put it in the cup.
Pour the boiling water over the leaves and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and drink it as needed to help relieve muscle pain.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "American journal of health-system pharmacy : AJHP : official journal of the American Society of Health-System Pharmacists", Ginkgo biloba teas may be potentially safe for alleviating muscle pain.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Ginkgo biloba teas can be used to treat muscle pain, but possible side effects may include dizziness, headaches, and stomach upset due to its blood-thinning properties. To use Ginkgo biloba teas safely, take it in moderation, monitor your bleeding time, and avoid consuming it before surgery or with other medications that thin the blood, such as aspirin or anticoagulants.
preparation_usage: Steep 1-2 teaspoons of Ginkgo biloba tea in a cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Drink 1-2 cups of Ginkgo biloba tea daily, 2-3 times a day.
Start with a low dose of 250-500mg of Ginkgo biloba per day.
Increase dose as needed, up to 1000mg per day, under doctor's supervision.
Continue using Ginkgo biloba tea for muscle pain relief for up to 2 weeks.

preparations.herb_desc: Achillea millefolium teas, also known as yarrow tea, have been traditionally used to treat muscle pain due to their anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The herbal preparation's ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain makes it an effective remedy for muscle soreness and stiffness. The bioactive constituents, such as flavonoids, sesquiterpenes, and coumarins, found in yarrow tea contribute to its therapeutic effects, including its ability to reduce muscle spasms and relieve pain. Regular consumption of Achillea millefolium teas may provide relief from muscle pain, reduce discomfort, and promote overall well-being, making it a beneficial herbal remedy for individuals suffering from this common ailment.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Achillea millefolium dried flowers and leaves.
Use 1/4 cup of the dried flowers and leaves for a strong tea.
Add 1 cup of boiling water over the dried flowers and leaves.
Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain it.
Drink 1 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day for muscle pain relief.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Achillea millefolium teas can be used to treat muscle pain due to its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, but possible side effects may include digestive issues such as nausea, diarrhea, and stomach upset. Precautions to take when using Achillea millefolium teas include avoiding excessive consumption, as it may lead to allergic reactions, interact with medications, and exacerbate conditions such as bleeding disorders, and pregnant or breastfeeding women should use it in moderation.
preparation_usage: Consult a healthcare professional for proper guidance on using Achillea millefolium tea.
Start with 1 teaspoon of dried Achillea millefolium per cup of boiling water.
Steep for 5-10 minutes, then strain and discard solids.
Drink 2-3 cups of tea per day, divided into 3-4 equal portions.
Use for 7-14 days, then reassess muscle pain and adjust frequency as needed.

preparations.herb_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas, also known as licorice root teas, have been traditionally used to treat muscle pain ailments such as muscle spasms and cramps. The anti-inflammatory properties of this herbal preparation help to reduce muscle inflammation and alleviate pain, making it an effective remedy for muscle pain relief. The bioactive constituents, including glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, in Glycyrrhiza glabra teas have been found to possess analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe muscle pain and promote relaxation. Regular consumption of Glycyrrhiza glabra teas has been associated with numerous benefits, including improved muscle function, reduced muscle stiffness, and enhanced overall well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Glycyrrhiza glabra root, 1 tablespoon of dried ginger, and 1 tablespoon of honey.
Boil 1 cup of water in a pot.
Add the dried Glycyrrhiza glabra root and ginger to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and simmer for 5-7 minutes. Strain the mixture into a cup.
Add honey to taste and drink the tea while warm.

preparations.preparation_side_effect: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas can be used to treat muscle pain, but possible side effects include water retention, high blood pressure, and low potassium levels due to its licorice root content. preparations.herb_desc: Salix alba teas have been traditionally used to treat muscle pain ailments, particularly those caused by inflammation and strain. preparations.preparation_side_effect: Salix alba teas can be used to treat muscle pain ailments, but possible side effects may include stomach upset, diarrhea, and allergic reactions in some individuals. Consume 1/4 teaspoon of dried Salix alba bark powder, 2-3 times a day.

Use Salix alba tea as a warm compress for muscle pain relief.
Combine Salix alba tea with other natural pain-relievers as directed.

preparations.herb_desc: Salvia officinalis teas have been traditionally used to treat muscle pain due to their anti-inflammatory, analgesic, and antispasmodic properties. The bioactive constituents such as rosmarinic acid and carnosic acid in Salvia officinalis teas help to reduce muscle spasms and inflammation, thereby alleviating pain and discomfort. The herbal preparation works by blocking the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and reducing the activity of pain-sensing nerve fibers, providing relief from muscle pain. The benefits of using Salvia officinalis teas for muscle pain treatment include reduced muscle spasms, improved mobility, and a decrease in the reliance on conventional pain medications.
preparation_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of fresh or dried Salvia officinalis leaves, 1 cup of boiling water, and 1 tablespoon of honey (optional).
Measure 1 cup of fresh or dried Salvia officinalis leaves and place them in a heat-resistant cup or teapot.
Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the leaves and let it steep for 5-7 minutes. Strain the tea if using fresh leaves.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey if desired for taste. Stir well to dissolve the honey.
Drink the tea warm or at room temperature, 2-3 times a day, as needed for muscle pain relief.
preparations.preparation_study: According to The Cochrane database of systematic reviews, Salvia officinalis teas for muscle pain were not evaluated in the study, as it focused on herbal medicines such as Harpagophytum Procumbens, Salix Alba, and Capsicum Frutescens for non-specific low-back pain.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Salvia officinalis teas can be used to treat muscle pain, but it may cause side effects such as dizziness, nausea, and stomach upset due to its high thujone content, which can interact with other medications and exacerbate certain health conditions. To minimize the risks, it is recommended to consume Salvia officinalis teas in moderation, monitor your body's response, and avoid using them if you have a history of stomach issues or are taking medications that may interact with thujone.

preparations.herb_desc: Arnica montana teas are a popular herbal remedy used to treat muscle pain ailments, thanks to their anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that help to reduce pain and inflammation in the affected areas. The bioactive constituents of Arnica montana, including flavonoids, sesquiterpenes, and phenolic acids, contribute to its therapeutic effects, helping to reduce muscle spasms and alleviate pain. By reducing inflammation and promoting blood flow to the affected areas, Arnica montana teas can help to speed up the healing process and alleviate muscle pain, making them a valuable natural remedy for athletes and individuals suffering from muscle strain. The benefits of using Arnica montana teas to treat muscle pain include reduced pain and inflammation, improved mobility, and enhanced recovery, making them a popular choice among those seeking a natural and effective solution.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of boiling water and 1 tablespoon of dried Arnica montana flowers.
Measure out 1/2 teaspoon of dried Arnica montana flowers and place it in a tea infuser or a piece of cheesecloth.
Pour the boiling water over the Arnica flowers and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the Arnica flowers.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day, as needed, to help with muscle pain relief.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Holistic nursing practice, Arnica montana teas for muscle pain have not been directly discussed in the provided study, but it mentions Arnica as one of the five common herbs reviewed, highlighting the need for more scientific studies on its efficacy and safety.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Arnica montana teas can be an effective remedy for muscle pain, but they may cause side effects such as dizziness, nausea, and stomach upset in some individuals due to their ability to thin the blood and stimulate the digestive system. To minimize potential side effects, it is essential to start with a low dose and gradually increase the amount as needed, and to avoid consuming these teas with other blood-thinning medications or substances that may interact with their active compounds.

preparations.herb_desc: Camellia sinensis teas have been traditionally used to alleviate muscle pain, with their analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties helping to reduce discomfort and inflammation. The herbal preparation's ability to relax muscles and improve blood flow contributes to its effectiveness in treating muscle pain. The bioactive constituents, such as theaflavins and thearubigins, found in Camellia sinensis teas have been shown to possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce muscle pain and inflammation. Regular consumption of Camellia sinensis teas can provide relief from muscle pain, promote relaxation, and improve overall well-being.
preparation_procedure: Measure 1 cup of Camellia sinensis tea leaves.
Boil 1 liter of water in a large pot.
Pour boiling water over the tea leaves and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a large cup and discard the tea leaves.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey and 1/2 cup of lemon juice to the tea for extra relief.
preparations.preparation_study: According to The American journal of Chinese medicine, Camellia sinensis teas have not been specifically studied for muscle pain relief in this scientific study, but it is mentioned as an ingredient in some medicated teas, which are used to relieve pain and modulate immunity.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Camellia sinensis teas can be used to treat muscle pain, but it may cause side effects such as dizziness, nausea, and stomach upset, especially when consumed in large quantities or for extended periods. To minimize these effects, individuals should start with a low dose, drink plenty of water, and avoid consuming it on an empty stomach, especially if they have sensitive stomachs or are prone to allergies.

preparations.herb_desc: Paeonia lactiflora teas, derived from the peony plant, have been traditionally used to treat muscle pain and inflammation, making them a popular herbal remedy for managing musculoskeletal discomfort. The anti-inflammatory properties of these teas are attributed to the presence of bioactive constituents such as glycosides, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which help to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain. The analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects of Paeonia lactiflora teas make them an effective treatment for muscle pain, allowing individuals to experience relief from discomfort and improved mobility. Regular consumption of these teas has been associated with various benefits, including reduced muscle spasms, improved joint flexibility, and enhanced overall well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 tablespoons of Paeonia lactiflora root or flowers.
Boil 1 cup of water in a pot.
Add the Paeonia lactiflora to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and drink 1/2 cup, 2-3 times a day for muscle pain relief.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Journal of ethnopharmacology, Paeonia lactiflora teas for muscle pain have been found to possess anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate muscle pain, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Paeonia lactiflora teas can be effective in treating muscle pain, but possible side effects include dizziness, stomach upset, and allergic reactions such as hives or itching. To minimize risks, individuals should start with a low dose, monitor their body's reaction, and avoid using this herbal preparation if they have a history of allergies or are taking medications that interact with Paeonia lactiflora, such as blood thinners or sedatives.

preparations.herb_desc: Corydalis turtschaninovii teas have been traditionally used to treat muscle pain, particularly in the context of rheumatoid arthritis and fibromyalgia. The analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties of this herbal preparation help to alleviate muscle pain by reducing inflammation and modulating pain perception. The bioactive constituents of Corydalis turtschaninovii teas, including protopine and bisnorcorydine, are responsible for its analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects. Regular consumption of Corydalis turtschaninovii teas has been found to provide relief from muscle pain, improve sleep quality, and enhance overall well-being, making it a valuable natural remedy for managing chronic muscle pain.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried Corydalis turtschaninovii root and 1 cup of water.
Boil the water in a pot and let it cool for 5 minutes.
Add the dried root to a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup.
Pour the cooled water over the root and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and drink 1-2 cups as needed to relieve muscle pain.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Zhong yao cai = Zhongyaocai = Journal of Chinese medicinal materials, Corydalis turtschaninovii teas may be effective for muscle pain as it contains tetrahydropalmatine, which has been found to promote the effect of preventing pain in rats.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Corydalis turtschaninovii teas can be effective in treating muscle pain, but they may also cause side effects such as dizziness, nausea, and stomach upset in some individuals. When using Corydalis turtschaninovii teas to treat muscle pain, it's essential to take precautions, such as starting with small doses, avoiding concurrent use with other medications, and not consuming them before or after surgery, as they may increase the risk of bleeding and other complications.

preparation_study: According to Current drug discovery technologies, Zingiber officinale teas for muscle pain have anti-inflammatory, analgesic, and antinociceptive properties, which may help manage sciatic pain.
intro_study: According to the study, teas for muscle pain may be effective due to green tea's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, particularly attributed to Epigallocatechin-3-gallate (EGCG), which helps suppress inflammation and oxidative stress.
